config_files.notes: Overwritten by the in-game Graphics menu and Rockstar Games Launcher on exit. Edit only while the game and launcher are fully closed.

keys.effect: Texture streaming quality and resident pool size: 0=normal, 1=high, 2=very high.
keys.notes: High (1) is suitable for 6 GB VRAM. Very High (2) requires 8 GB+ for stable frame times in busy areas like downtown Los Santos.

keys.effect: Shadow map resolution and cascade count: 0=normal, 1=high, 2=very high, 3=ultra.
keys.notes: The highest single GPU cost setting in GTA V. Dropping from Ultra to High typically recovers 10-20% GPU frame time in open-world driving scenarios. Ultra is rarely justified at any hardware tier.

keys.effect: Reflection probe quality used on vehicles and surfaces: 0=normal, 1=high, 2=very high, 3=ultra.
keys.notes: Ultra has a disproportionate VRAM cost versus visual gain. Very High (2) is the practical ceiling for quality mode.
GTA V's open-world renderer makes MSAA extremely expensive — x4 and x8 are not recommended even on high-end hardware. FXAA provides acceptable smoothing at minimal cost.

keys.effect: Grass density, physics, and draw distance in rural areas: 0=normal, 1=high, 2=very high, 3=ultra.
keys.notes: High GPU and CPU cost in rural San Andreas areas (Blaine County, Mount Chiliad). Ultra grass is rarely visible at racing speeds. Reducing from Ultra to High is a reliable win on any hardware tier.

keys.effect: Material shading complexity for lighting and surface rendering: 0=normal, 1=high, 2=very high.
Post-processing quality including depth of field, motion blur, and lens effects: 0=normal, 1=high, 2=very high, 3=ultra.
keys.notes: Ultra PostFX enables high-quality depth of field and lens flare. Motion blur is included in this setting — if motion blur causes discomfort, set PostFX to Normal or disable MotionBlurStrength separately.
Default is enabled. Motion blur is a common trigger for motion discomfort, particularly during high-speed vehicle travel. Recommended off for all settings.

engine_overrides.behaviour: The entire settings.xml is rewritten whenever the in-game Graphics menu is closed or the Rockstar Games Launcher syncs settings. Manual edits will be lost if the game or launcher is open.
engine_overrides.action: Instruct the user to close both GTA V and the Rockstar Games Launcher before editing settings.xml, and to make changes before launching the game.

manual_only_settings.recommendation: Reduce to 25-50% on performance hardware to significantly lower CPU overhead in populated areas.
manual_only_settings.reason: NPC and vehicle population density is a major CPU cost factor — more impactful than most GPU settings on CPU-limited systems.

manual_only_settings.recommendation: Reduce to 50% on performance hardware. Does not affect density — only NPC model variety.
manual_only_settings.reason: High variety increases asset streaming load. Lower values reduce RAM pressure without reducing apparent crowd density.

manual_only_settings.recommendation: 0 on performance/balanced hardware. Increase only if VRAM exceeds 10 GB.
manual_only_settings.reason: Increases the distance at which detail objects and buildings are rendered at full quality. High VRAM cost with marginal visual benefit at ground level.

notes: GTA V Enhanced Edition (2022 remaster on PC) significantly improved GPU performance and added ray tracing support. Ensure users are running the Enhanced Edition — it replaced the original on Steam and Rockstar Launcher automatically.
GTA Online imposes server-side limits on some settings. Settings changed in GTA Online may not match single-player settings.xml values.
NVIDIA DLSS and AMD FSR are available in GTA V Enhanced Edition through the in-game menu under Anti-Aliasing. These are not present in the pre-2022 version.
The settings.xml file may be reset to defaults if the Rockstar Games Launcher detects a version mismatch or cache corruption.
